message:
fix(io): add fail-closed completion guards to all AsyncReadExt futures

Add a `completed` boolean field to every future returned by
AsyncReadExt and AsyncReadVectoredExt trait methods. After poll
returns Ready (success or error), subsequent polls return
io::Error::other("... polled after completion") instead of
re-driving the inner reader.

Affected futures (all in src/io/ext/read_ext.rs):
- Read: single read operation
- ReadVectored: scatter/gather read
- ReadExact: exact-length fill with yield budgeting
- ReadToEnd: growable buffer read-to-EOF
- ReadToString: UTF-8 validated read-to-EOF
- ReadU8, ReadI8: single-byte reads
- read_int_future! macro (ReadU16..ReadI128): multi-byte integer reads

Each future sets completed=true on every terminal path (success, error,
UnexpectedEof) and checks it at poll entry. The macro-generated integer
read futures use concat!(stringify!($future), " future polled after
completion") for per-type error messages.

This prevents subtle bugs where a completed read future is accidentally
repolled and either re-drives the reader (consuming additional data) or
returns stale buffer contents.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
